Why Families Choose Integrity Closings CLT for Bedside Notarizations

When someone you love is in the hospital or a nursing facility, you don't just need a notary — you need someone who understands the weight of the moment. Here's why Charlotte families keep calling us back.

Serving hospitals, nursing homes, and care facilities across Mecklenburg and Union Counties.

Call to Check Availability Request a Same-Day Visit

Patient, compassionate visits
Clear communication throughout
Why This Service Is Different

Bedside Notarizations Require More Than a Notary Stamp

Most people have never needed a notary at a hospital or nursing home before. It's not like stopping into a UPS store. The signer may be anxious, in pain, or exhausted. The family may be overwhelmed. Time may be short.

That's why choosing the right notary for this kind of visit matters. A professional who shows up calm, prepared, and genuinely respectful of the situation makes a real difference — not just in getting the document done, but in how the whole experience feels for everyone in that room.

What You Can Expect

What Families and Caregivers Value Most

🤝

Calm, Unhurried Presence

We move at the patient's pace. If the signer needs a moment, we give them one. There's no rushing, no impatience — the signing happens when the patient is ready.

📋

Clear Communication Before & After

We confirm everything before we arrive — documents, ID, witnesses, access protocols. After the visit, you'll know exactly what was completed and what your next step is.

🏥

Experience With Facility Environments

We know how to work within hospitals and nursing homes — checking in at the desk, following visitor protocols, and working around care schedules without disrupting the patient's routine.

🔒

Honest and Professional

If something isn't right — the signer seems unclear, the document has issues, the ID doesn't match — we say so. We won't push through a notarization we have concerns about. That protects you and your loved one.

📍

Local and Reachable

Integrity Closings CLT is a Charlotte-area business, not a national platform. When you call, you're talking directly to Frank — the notary who will show up at the facility. No dispatching. No uncertainty about who's coming.

⏱️

Responsive When It Matters

Situations at hospitals and nursing homes don't always give you days to plan. We work to accommodate urgent requests and same-day visits when our schedule allows.

What Families Tell Us

What Makes This Experience Easier for Families

After working with families at hospitals and care facilities across the Charlotte area, these are the things that consistently matter most to the people we serve.

  • 1

    Knowing who's coming

    When you schedule with us, you know Frank Lamon Coxx is coming. A real name, a local number, and a professional who takes this work seriously.

  • 2

    Not having to explain the situation twice

    We listen carefully on the first call and arrive prepared. You don't have to re-explain everything at the door of the facility.

  • 3

    Respectful treatment of seniors

    We speak directly to the patient — not just over them or around them. Dignity matters in this work, and we don't forget that.

  • 4

    Transparency about what we can and can't do

    We tell families the truth. If a document can't proceed because of a capacity concern or a missing ID, we explain why clearly and without judgment.

  • 5

    One less thing to manage

    When you're already navigating medical decisions, family logistics, and legal paperwork — having a reliable notary who handles their side smoothly is one genuine relief.

  • 6

    Availability when it's actually needed

    Medical situations don't wait for Monday. We do our best to be available when families need us — not just during standard business hours.

About the Notary

You're Working With Frank Lamon Coxx — Directly

Integrity Closings CLT is a Charlotte-based mobile notary business owned and operated by Frank Lamon Coxx. When you call, you speak with Frank. When we arrive at the facility, it's Frank. There's no middleman, no dispatching to a stranger, no guessing who's going to show up.

Frank specializes in bedside notarizations at hospitals, nursing homes, assisted living, and rehabilitation facilities throughout the Charlotte metro area — including Mint Hill, Monroe, Matthews, and surrounding communities.

Our Commitments

What You Can Always Count On

  • We arrive at the time we confirmed — or we call you if anything changes
  • We follow all facility check-in and visitor protocols without complaint
  • We introduce ourselves clearly to the patient before proceeding
  • We assess the signer's awareness and willingness with care and judgment
  • We explain the process in plain language — no unnecessary legal jargon
  • We will not complete a notarization we have legitimate concerns about
  • We treat every patient with the same dignity regardless of their condition
  • We communicate clearly if something cannot proceed and explain the next step
FAQ

Questions Families Ask Before They Call

How do I know the notary will handle this professionally?
Frank Lamon Coxx is a licensed North Carolina Notary Public who specializes in bedside notarizations at hospitals and nursing facilities. When you call, you speak directly with Frank — not a call center. His approach is calm, patient, and respectful of the patient's condition and the family's stress. We'd encourage you to call and ask any questions you have before scheduling.
What happens if the notarization can't be completed?
We'll tell you clearly what happened and why. The most common reasons a notarization can't proceed are: the signer cannot be adequately identified, the signer appears unable to understand what they are signing, or the signer is not acting voluntarily. In each case, we explain the situation honestly and, where possible, suggest a next step — such as consulting an elder law attorney or rescheduling for a better time.
Is this a good fit for an elderly parent who is nervous or easily confused?
It depends on the level of confusion. If your parent is anxious but mentally aware and able to understand what they're signing, yes — we take extra time, speak calmly, and make the experience as comfortable as possible. If there are significant memory or cognition concerns, call us first so we can discuss the situation honestly before scheduling a visit.
Do you handle urgent situations — like a patient who may not have much time?
Yes. These are among the most important calls we receive. Call 980-505-8050 directly and explain the urgency. We will do everything we can to arrange a same-day visit. In sensitive situations, please also confirm with the attending medical team that the patient is currently in a state where notarization can proceed.
Can you also work with facility social workers or case managers?
Yes. We're comfortable coordinating directly with social workers, case managers, discharge planners, and administrative staff. If you're a professional arranging a notary visit on behalf of a resident or patient, call us and we'll work with your scheduling needs and facility protocols.
What if I have questions you haven't answered here?
Just call. Frank is glad to talk through your situation before you commit to anything. 980-505-8050 — no obligation, no pressure, just a direct conversation to make sure we're the right fit for your needs.

© Integrity Closings CLT · Frank Lamon Coxx, Notary Public · Charlotte, NC

Disclaimer: Integrity Closings CLT provides mobile notary services only and does not provide legal advice. Notarization requires the signer to be mentally aware, willing, and in possession of valid government-issued photo ID. Completion is subject to the notary's professional judgment.
